reviews.text:
It's hard to put in words how incredibly grateful and thankful I am for Matt Fendon. I sustained a very serious neck injury at work and really, really didn't want to "lawyer up". I spent 4 month misdiagnosed w/a shoulder injury, the insurance company (IC) refused my request for a 2nd opinion. Eventually, I was allowed to see a Pain Management doctor (PMD)...after the shoulder specialist admitted that he didn't know what was wrong; as I was supposed to be "fully healed" in 3 months, but still in excruciating pain after that timeframe.

At my first PMD visit she said "I'm 95% sure it's your neck. 100% it isn't your shoulder". She was right. I was crushed. Once that was confirmed, IC approve dang near every non-surgical procedures in an attempt to avoid the huge cost of surgery on THREE vertebrae! After months of being poked, prodded and plied with medication IC finally allowed a consultation with a neck surgeon. He advocated to move forward with surgery, being the only true solution to fix the damage Before the the IC would approve it I had to be examined by an IME; which apparently is a normal part of the process, but weird considering all the doctors I'd been examined by were their doctors. When I read the IME's reviews it was abundantly clear that his job was to deny high cost procedures. I tried working with IC regarding the IME, but my reasonable requests were denied. This was my breaking point.

Thankfully, about a month prior, I had researched lawyers in preparation for this worst case scenerio. In my (ridiculously thorough) research on AZ WC lawyers I came across Matt Fendon Law Group and when I spoke with Matt I instantly knew he was who I'd want to represent me if necessary. He was so patient in listening to the details of my situation; which was so appreciated as he knew I was at a point where I was trying to avoid getting a lawyer unless I had to. In turn, he explained how representation works (I've never hired a lawyer before): fee structure, services his practice provides, petition options for various aspects of the system, etc. And he finished with something along the lines of, "I know you need time to decide if this is the route you want to take. I'm not a used car salesman, I won't harass you with calls, but I will place you in the system as a client I would like to represent, so if you do call we will be ready to help you". This statement was so honorable to me....and he was true to his word! About a month later was when I determined in order to get the surgery I NEEDED I had to hire a professional to guide me through this totally complicated/corrupt system. I'd handled it on my own for far too long. Incredibly, Matt successfully fought to have surgery approved and was under the knife about a month after him taking on my case.

The decision to obtain legal representation was the best decision I've made in this mess. Since the surgery,  so many things have come up that would have totally overwhelmed me without his guidance. He has expressed a genuine concern for my mental wellbeing and given me hope that it could be an area covered by WC. These mental health struggles have been documented by at least 2 of their doctors and completely ignored.

Currently, IC is pushing to close my case, even though I continue to have nerve pain from a nerve that was smashed in my neck for the 8 months IC tried to avoid paying for surgery. This damage still causes significant pain in my right shoulder and at times makes my right arm and hand go numb...7 month post surgery. MFLG is now fighting to get me get continued care and treatment for the damaged nerve; which I hope and pray can even be fixed. I can't even fathom living with nerve pain for the rest of my life. While there are no promises that it is even fixable, I will at least have the peace of mind that we tried everything possible to fix it.

Most importantly, Matt has helped me to get my "fight" back. I was so beat down after trying to work alone with the IC for 6/7 months, now I feel like I can stand up for myself again because I have someone who has my back.

After years of unsuccessful care, not knowing how the system worked, I was told by comp "case closed!" Stunned with no help I discovered Matt traveled to Flagstaff. He immediately began to solve the issue & re-open my case. Without his expert advice I would still be suffering. He not only got me the care I needed but made my wife & I feel like family. We highly urge anyone in need to contact Matt. Our sincere thanks. John & Amy D

reviews.text:
Contacted the law office of Tevis Reich for a consultation about an issue we have with a contractor. They asked who the parties were to verify if a conflict of interest existed before setting up an appointment with us.

We live in Williams about 45 minutes away from Flagstaff. When we arrive for the appointment, they ask us to fill out paperwork we had already completed on-line. A few minutes later, Tevis came out of his office and said there was a conflict of interest that would prevent him from meeting with us. He wouldn’t tell us anything about the conflict of interest. Just that they made a mistake and he couldn’t meet with us. Really? I had to take half a day off of work, and drive for 1.5 hours for nothing because they were careless and all he can say is “I’m sorry”.

Avoid this guy unless you like to be jerked around.

Edit to reply to Tevis’ response below:

Tevis is trying to make it sound like it was our fault. My wife called up their office and provided the names of all parties. The person who took the information, I think her name was Zoe, checked with Tevis and told us there was no conflict of interest. Five minutes after we arrived for the appointment he comes out to say there is a conflict of interest and he can’t talk with us, or disclose the conflict.
So either he didn’t bother to look at the information we had provided days before until the five minutes after we arrived for our scheduled appointment, or he never bothered to inform us he would need to cancel the appointment due to a conflict before we drove the 45 minutes to Flagstaff. Either way it was very unprofessional.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We are sorry that you feel the identification of a legal conflict of interest to be a process in which you were jerked around. Unfortunately, this is part of the process when you are looking at hiring a lawyer or law firm. I wish you the best of luck in finding an attorney you can work with.

For anyone looking at hiring an attorney, it is important to spell names correctly and fully to be most effective in this process. Sometimes a conflict may be realized even later in the process making things even more awkward and challenging. It is noted that an unsolicited email to this office through the internet is not part of our intake process or client screening (and there is no online form offered to be filled out).

I cannot recommend Michael Wozniak of Aspey, Watkins & Diesel, PLLC highly enough for his exceptional work in successfully managing my complex international Hague Convention repatriation case. Highly recommended by and coordinating seamlessly with the U.S. Department of State and Italian Central Authority, his tireless dedication, meticulous attention to detail, and efficient approach were exceptionally instrumental in securing the return of my very young child, who had been wrongfully retained by my spouse in Arizona without my consent. Coordinating with him both virtually from abroad in Italy and in person for the hearing itself in Flagstaff, Arizona, his demeanour was consistently highly professional, providing lucid and reassuring communication throughout the process.
Mr. Wozniak’s expertise in navigating both international and Arizona law, with his clear, strategic guidance tailored to my personal situation and documentation, made all the difference in this rare and fact-intensive case. According to the U.S. Department of State’s 2024 Annual Report on International Child Abduction, approximately 1,000 Hague Convention cases are processed annually worldwide. The success rate for judicially ordered returns in favor of the left-behind parent is only 43%, according to the 2015 HCCH survey regarding the 326 return applications processed in the U.S. Against these challenging odds, Mr. Wozniak’s phenomenal legal team handled the delicate nature of the proceedings with utmost professionalism, ensuring every aspect was thoroughly addressed. Without their outstanding efforts, I firmly believe the return of my child would not have been granted. Mr. Wozniak and his team are truly remarkable, and I am deeply grateful for their life-changing support.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Edoardo, for your incredibly thoughtful review. We’re honored to have played a role in such a deeply personal and challenging matter. In the beginning I had a difficult time getting ahold of him and was told by his staff that his focus was on clients who had already retained him....I should had looked else where from that point but I decided to give him a chance. Once I finally spoke to him i stressed my concern on several factors, his credibility, and availability and professionalism. I was assured that those wouldnt be an issue if I retained him. I informed him I like him to 1st meet my friend who was in jail so that he can advise me if he want to retain this attny. Upon my request he meet my friend and offered an affordable rate which I was very appreciative for. He gave me 10 days to make the pymt but to show how serious I was I made that payment the following morning. His staff said Id receive a email within an hour confirming my payment. By that afternoon I sent an email to follow up to make sure that he was aware that I made the payment but no confirmation. He never responded, that following Sunday I sent another email (not expecting him to respond on that day but at least by the following business day morning) regarding question on my friend behalf. Still no call or email so by that afternoon I sent a 3rd email and he responded instantly. The tone in that email made me feel like I upset him for the 3 emails (mind you he advise me that if I called and didnt get him to email him for I wld get a faster response). I followed up to that email with an apology and to just let him know I was just making sure I was on track w/ everything and to get a question answered. The following day I received a important letter that he wanted me to confirm I got so I faxed it to him. I didnt get a response so I called to confirm they got it and his secretary said he did. I also ask to setup an appt for him to call my friend whom had been trying to get ahold of him and had no luck. I was then informed he was going on vacation for the rest of the week and wldnt be available til some time the following week and by the way my friend court date was continued. That was news to me. I relayed that info to my friend who has now been in jail for a week and a half with no clue of whats going on. I call back to request what documents had been filed and thats when his staff aka his wife informed me they decided they didnt want to do business with my friend because she didnt like the fact that he calls everyday asking questions and demanding answers and now he's becoming rude and disrespectful, and that they will refund me back my funds.  Im shocked at her response and tried to plead with her and she wasn't caring at all. I was so stressed out I emailed Lee that night to apologize again and plead with him and try to explain the lack of communication and amount of stress we were under. Needless to say he didnt respond back but the next day his secretary sent me a email saying this is a confirmation of your funds being credited back to your card.  Their action and how they handle business from my experience is nothing like what Ive read. I felt like my friend case was not important on their priority list which was very disheartening and waste of our time. Now I have to start over with my search and it doesn't look like my friend will be home for Christmas. I was really hoping to leave a great review like some of the others I read but our customer satisfaction was mislead. ... Good luck to those in the future.

Brian Webb is an absolute gem in this area of Arizona. He’s the first attorney I’ve spoke with about my case that really gave me the impression that he genuinely cares about what is going on in my case. In his free consultation with me today, which was supposed to be hour but went over an extra 30 minutes, he gave me a lot of pointers and invaluable advice that I could use immediately to benefit and advance my case along. For free. He explained how the process will go and what I needed to do to for service to make sure it holds up in court, as well as directed me to legal rules that I need to understand in order to move forward intelligently. While I won’t be hiring for full representation (although if I was made of money I would because he is worth it!) his background and attentiveness to the nature of my situation has made me extremely comfortable to hire his a la cart legal services to help me prepare my case to represent myself. He offered me a great deal to block off a half day to spend with him before my case to get me up to speed and help me make sure my case is strongly prepared in accordance to all the rules and expectations the court has for my type of case.
He has already made me feel more at ease and acquainted with the process, and I’m looking forward to working with him for a solid 3 to 4 hours the week before my hearing. He spent time with me to make sure he understands the ins and outs of my situation before giving out any advice, and out of all the lawyers I spoke with about my situation he’s the only one that made me feel like he actually cares. I can’t recommend Brian Webb enough, he knows his stuff and knows about the unique struggles of my kind of juvenile case in rural Arizona and I’m confident that with a little bit of his guidance he can help me make, and present, a great case. It took a lot of calling around for me to find him but I’m glad I didn’t settle and kept looking. I hope my review helps other people waste less time shopping around, good lawyers are a dime a dozen, but great lawyers that care are rare. Brian will work with your situation, not just to understand it, but also offer you alternatives to work with your budget and still get what you need.

reviews.text: I had a horrible experience with 928 Law. Taylor Swick and Brian Webb were deceitful and completely unprepared. I had to make a quick decision on hiring representation for a very stressful custody battle and was so confident after the consultation but shortly after was extremely disappointed. I explained the situation, that my previous attorney was being disbarred for various reasons including bailing on several clients, that my children were dealing with extreme parental alienation, and that I myself was dealing with harassment and constant threats. Nothing that was proposed over the phone was ever done. Swick assured me that he would first file for a protection order, but never did. Nothing was ever filed in regards to the need for an order of contempt to fail to follow the current parenting agreement, nothing was even done until a few weeks before the trial which was also when I was made aware that I would not be allowed to use evidence or witnesses, although I was getting billed for the research on this as well as almost 30 attempts to contact the previous lawyer who was not reachable (and Swick was well aware of this). I was billed for conversations with Webb that I was promised I wouldn’t be billed for, conversations that went on over an hour of him rambling trying to convince me that they’re doing their job but not allowing me to get a word in. The day of the trial was so unorganized that the other party even tried to help Swick coordinate which recording with the corresponding transcript- none of it was used because of his unprofessional embarrassment. He showed up late, sweaty palms, not a single objection or argument was successful, or made much less, and in the end I was billed $30,000.  If you’re looking for dishonesty and outrageous billing, you’re in the right place.

I really enjoyed working with this law firm. The folks I worked with were very kind and empathetic. Brian Webb was really attentive, informative, and went above and beyond. I would reccomend this firm 100% to anyone needing legal services.
